QF.id: XIII Trading, Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_6

question.text: How are currency derivatives contracts settled in India?

answer.text: All exchange-traded currency derivatives are cash settled in Indian Rupees — daily settlement on T+1 and final settlement on T+2
All currency derivatives are physically settled through actual delivery of foreign currency
USDINR is physically settled while cross-currency pairs are cash settled
Currency futures are cash settled but currency options require physical delivery

explanation.text: **The Logic:** All ETCD contracts in India are cash settled. Daily mark-to-market is settled on T+1 (next working day). Final settlement at expiry is on T+2 (two business days after last trading day). Cross-currency profit/loss is calculated in quote currency but settled in INR. **The Trap:** Physical delivery of foreign currency requires RBI approval and is not done on exchanges. Both futures and options are cash settled in the ETCD segment. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I Ch7 covers currency settlement. Series VIII covers equity settlement (which can be physical for stock F&O). Series XIII compares settlement mechanisms across segments. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Cash settlement eliminates delivery risk but introduces settlement price risk — the final settlement price may differ slightly from the closing spot rate.
